name: Mandayona - Sigüenza – circular por las hoces del río Dulce hasta cascada Gollorio
region_intro: Mountain biking around Mandayona, located in the province of Guadalajara within Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, offers diverse landscapes and natural features. The region is characterized by varied terrain, including the gorges of the Dulce River and areas with natural singletrack trails. Riders can expect a mix of challenging ascents and scenic paths through significant natural beauty. Best mountain bike trails around Mandayona The most popular mountain bike trail is Mandayona - Sigüenza – circular por las hoces del río Dulce hasta cascada Gollorio, a 35.3 miles (56.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 51 minutes to complete. This route goes through the gorges of the Dulce River and reaches the Gollorio Waterfall. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pelegrina Castle – Riverside Rest Area loop from Castillo de Pelegrina, a difficult 34.4 miles (55.3 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and scenic views of the surrounding natural landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Riverside Rest Area – Pelegrina Castle loop from Aragosa, a 15.9 miles (25.6 km) trail leading through the Río Dulce Ravine Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Mountain biking around Mandayona is defined by the Río Dulce Ravine Natural Park, the Gollorio Waterfall, and varied terrain with natural singletrack trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Mandayona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Mandayona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Mandayona?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Mandayona, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. The komoot community has explored these routes over 60 times, highlighting the area's diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Mandayona?
A: Mountain biking in Mandayona features varied terrain, including natural singletrack trails and paths through the gorges of the Dulce River. You'll encounter a mix of challenging ascents and scenic routes, particularly within the Río Dulce Ravine Natural Park.

Q: Are there any difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Mandayona offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004666892/mandayona-sigueenza-circular-por-las-hoces-del-rio-dulce-hasta-cascada-gollorio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mandayona - Sigüenza – circular por las hoces del río Dulce hasta cascada Gollorio&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 56.8 km trail that takes nearly 5 hours to complete, traversing the gorges of the Dulce River and reaching the Gollorio Waterfall. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3972201" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pelegrina Castle – Riverside Rest Area loop from Castillo de Pelegrina&lt;/a&gt;, a 55.3 km path with varied terrain.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in the region are rated as moderate. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10416115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside Rest Area – Pelegrina Castle loop from Aragosa&lt;/a&gt;, a 25.6 km trail through the Río Dulce Ravine Natural Park, typically completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Río Dulce – Riverside Rest Area loop from Torremocha del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 36.6 km long.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see while mountain biking in Mandayona?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3399726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollorio Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, especially active from autumn to spring, along routes like the Mandayona - Sigüenza circular trail. The Río Dulce Ravine Natural Park itself offers spectacular views and unique geological formations like the Rocks of the Friars (Peñas de los Frailes).

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible from the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area around Mandayona is dotted with historical sites. You might pass by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3399704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pelegrina Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3972201" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pelegrina Castle – Riverside Rest Area loop&lt;/a&gt;. The majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3967544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigüenza Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3967563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigüenza Castle&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby, offering significant historical and artistic interest.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that are loop routes?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Mandayona are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004666892/mandayona-sigueenza-circular-por-las-hoces-del-rio-dulce-hasta-cascada-gollorio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mandayona - Sigüenza – circular por las hoces del río Dulce hasta cascada Gollorio&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19811842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajera Reservoir loop from Las Inviernas&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.8 km moderate route.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Mandayona?
A: The mountain bike trails in Mandayona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the scenic paths through the Río Dulce Ravine Natural Park, and the variety of terrain, including natural singletrack sections.

Q: Is Mandayona a good destination for mountain biking with family?
A: While the region offers a mix of moderate to difficult trails, there are options that can be enjoyed by families with some mountain biking experience. It's advisable to check the specific difficulty and length of trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10416115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside Rest Area – Pelegrina Castle loop from Aragosa&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 25.6 km route, to ensure it matches your family's skill level.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Mandayona?
A: The Gollorio Waterfall, a prominent feature in the region, is most active from autumn to spring, suggesting these seasons offer lush scenery. However, the diverse terrain and natural beauty can be enjoyed throughout much of the year, depending on weather conditions. Always check local forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by a reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19811842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajera Reservoir loop from Las Inviernas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 33.8 km mountain bike trail that takes you around the Tajera Reservoir, offering scenic views of the water and surrounding landscapes.
tips: Restored rich spring fountain with rest area in the chopera and close to the municipality of Mandayona.

The Río Dulce ravine was one of my first mountain bike tours. The setting is amazing and charming. The ride is easy and all the scenery, both inside and outside the canyon, is simply spectacular. I was especially struck by the clarity of the river waters and the life seen through them in the form of algae and fish.

Pelegrina is a reference enclave of the Barranco del Río Dulce natural park. It is located on or at the top of one of its gorges and has a privileged view of the gorges that flank the canyon. Its urban complex is very well preserved and the ruins of the castle govern the entire town and the ravine from the top of a hill. Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente established his camp here in 1970 during the recording of some of the chapters of El hombre y la tierra.

Waterfall located in the bed of the Gollorio ravine, a tributary of the Dulce River, within the natural park of the Río Dulce Ravine. It is about fifty meters high and only appears from autumn to spring when the rains fill the sources that supply the bed of the ravine.

Just a few meters from the road and the parking lot is this viewpoint with beautiful views of the Río Dulce ravine and the Gollorio ravine where downstream is the wonderful homonymous waterfall that is worth visiting.
Beautiful viewpoint over the town of Sigüenza. It is accessed by a somewhat broken track right on the ascent ramp

waypoints.name: Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente Viewpoint – Dulce River Canyon
Mirador del Cid Viewpoint in Sigüenza
